posts.content: Jhonata Diniz vs. José Luiz lets gooo the fight weve all been dying to see

posts.author: elitemmapicks
posts.content:
[Predictions]

Jhonata Diniz vs José Luiz
Pick: Diniz via round 1 KO
94% of Tapology users (47 users) are picking Diniz (86% KO)
Luiz is 5.2 years younger (29.8 vs 35.0)
Luiz’s reach is not published
Diniz has +0.27 strike differential/min
Diniz has 1.3% knockdown rate, 0% knocked down rate
Luiz proj. 51.9% finish chance vs 31.8% and 16.2% decision
Luiz only has 7 career fights vs a lower level of competition
Luiz’s most recent opponent was 3-0, fight before that opponent was 1-0
7/9 wins for Diniz were via KO
6/7 KO’s for Diniz were in round 1
Luiz has 2:0 KO win/loss ratio, and 4:0 submission win/loss ratio
Luiz is a good play if he is significant betting favorite
Diniz will likely win striking and get the KO

To make a few points

Kape’s recent loss was a snoozer against Mokaev, a top 10 flyweight whos never fighting in the UFC again.Goonie’s most recent loss was against Patchy Mix, who everyone thought was a top 5 BW before he got exposed.Goonie might be the more experienced fighter, but he’s the more war-torn guy between him and Kape.
Kape by Fraudcheck

posts.author: elitemmapicks
posts.content:
[Predictions]

Manel Kape vs Kyoji Horiguchi
Pick: Kape via round 3 KO
36% of Tapology users (106 users) are picking Horiguchi (78% KO)
Kape is 3.1 years younger (32.6 vs 35.7)
Kape has 5” reach edge (68” vs 63”)
Horiguchi has +1.64 strike differential/min vs +0.96
Horiguchi has +1.17 strike differential/min @distance vs +0.73
Horiguchi proj. +34.4 significant strikes attempted
Horiguchi proj. +12.7 significant strikes landed
Horiguchi proj. +7.3 head strikes landed
Kape has 49% head strike accuracy, 70% head strike defense
Horiguchi has 41% head strike accuracy, 72% head strike defense
Horiguchi proj. 0.54 knockdowns vs 0.51
Horiguchi proj. 2.0 takedowns landed (6.7 attempts) vs 0.8 (2.2 attempts)
Horiguchi has 15:19 takedown ratio, he spent 23% of fight time in control, 19% being controlled
Kape has 3:7 takedown ratio, he spent 3% of fight time in control, 7% being controlled
Horiguchi has 1:8 submission attempt ratio in UFC (100% secured, 12% conceded)
Kape has 2:1 submission attempt ratio in UFC (0% secured, 0% conceded)
Kape proj. 36.3% finish chance vs 28.4% and 35.2% decision
Horiguchi submitted Kape via arm triangle in December 2017 in RIZIN
Horiguchi has 15:2 KO win/loss ratio (1/2 times KO’d in round 1, 1/2 in round 4)
Kape has 14:0 KO win/loss ratio (8/14 KO’s in round 1, 3/14 in round 2, 3/14 in round 3)
Horiguchi is a good play if he is significant betting favorite
Horiguchi will likely win striking by small amount but has way more get finished downside
Horiguchi likely won’t be able to control Kape enough

* Edited at 03.24.2026, 11:54 PM ET *

posts.author: MarkusB
posts.content:
[Predictions]

The UFC has consistently been making the worst decisions possible as of late, in what world do you put a rematch of a fight that sold over 18 thousand tickets out in japan in the apex in front of no crowd.

posts.author: B7L7
posts.content:
[Predictions]

Last time they fought Kyoji showed levels. He was one step ahead and just faster in every single striking exchange. That said, he’s still fast but is 8 years older, and Kape has improved drastically. Kape is also so much bigger than he was back then, and is on that Ali Abdelaziz/Dagestani gear. It should be a different fight this time around.

* Edited at 03.26.2026, 12:00 PM ET *

posts.author: battousai
posts.content:
This rematch was made for a reason and it's because the UFC cannot stand Manel Kape and want him out of the title picture ASAP. Kyoji Horiguchi has been the uncrowned featherweight champion for going on a decade now. No doubt Kape is better compared to the first time they fought, but so is Horiguchi.

Don't see this fight going any different than the first meeting and I think there's a very good chance the UFC is thinking the same.

posts.author: patrickmoore_1
posts.content:
[Predictions]

@battousai yeah im sure the ufc cant stand one of the most entertaining fighters in the ufc and one of the only fighters that actually tries to sell fights. According to kape the ufc said he could wait for a title shot but he chose this fight instead, kapes going to smoke kyogi
